reasoning: Boris Johnson is currently not an MP, having resigned his seat in June 2023. Kemi Badenoch was recently elected as the Conservative Party Leader in November 2024. For Johnson to return as leader before January 1, 2026, he would need to find a vacant seat, win a by-election to get back into Parliament (with the approval of Badenoch and party leadership, which is highly unlikely), and then successfully mount a leadership challenge to oust Badenoch—all within roughly 8 months (May to Dec 2025). The most prominent upcoming by-election in Runcorn and Helsby has already seen Sean Houlston chosen as the Conservative candidate, quashing February speculation that Johnson might run there. Without an impending by-election candidacy and facing a very tight 2025 timeline, there is no viable path for his return by January 1, 2026. Therefore, the probability is set to the floor value (0.02).
